activation_results.reason: Verb swap preserves the canonical request for website copy across the same pages.
SSH configuration is unrelated to website copywriting.
Meta question about skills, not a request to write website copy.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'optimise', 'optimised', 'humour' — no banned US spellings detected.

criterion_results.text: Output is machine-readable where the description implies (valid JSON/SQL/YAML)
criterion_results.evidence: Description implies prose copy in markdown, not structured data; markdown is well-formed.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "0. Site Overview", "1. Homepage"
criterion_results.evidence: Both '## 0. Site Overview' and '## 1. Homepage' headings present in the artefact.

judge_result.notes: Artefact cleanly meets all three criteria. Australian spellings used throughout (optimise, humour), canonical section headings present, and the markdown structure is appropriate for the prose deliverable implied by the skill description. Activation rules align with the description: positive cases (canonical, paraphrase, name-mention) clearly fire; negative cases (SSH, meta query about skills) clearly do not.
